description: Display plugin icons in marketplace search results. The opentabs-plugin build command now embeds iconSvg and iconDarkSvg into each plugin's package.json opentabs field. The MCP server needs to fetch these icons from the npm registry when returning search results, pass them through to the browser extension, and the side panel UI needs to render them in NpmPluginCard instead of the current letter-avatar fallback.

userStories.title: Add iconSvg and iconDarkSvg fields to PluginSearchResult and fetch from npm registry
userStories.description: Add iconSvg and iconDarkSvg fields to the PluginSearchResult type in both the MCP server and browser extension bridge. Update searchNpmPlugins to fetch full package metadata from the npm registry to retrieve these icon fields from the opentabs section of package.json.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginSearchResult in platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts has iconSvg: string and iconDarkSvg: string fields
PluginSearchResult in platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts has iconSvg: string and iconDarkSvg: string fields
searchNpmPlugins returns icon data fetched from the npm registry package metadata
Icons are fetched from the opentabs.iconSvg and opentabs.iconDarkSvg fields of each package's registry metadata
Plugins without icons get empty string values for iconSvg and iconDarkSvg

userStories.title: Render marketplace icons in NpmPluginCard
userStories.description: Update NpmPluginCard to pass iconSvg and iconDarkSvg from the search result to the PluginIcon component, replacing the letter-avatar fallback with actual plugin icons.
acceptanceCriteria: NpmPluginCard passes plugin.iconSvg as iconSvg prop to PluginIcon
NpmPluginCard passes plugin.iconDarkSvg as iconDarkSvg prop to PluginIcon
PluginIcon renders the SVG icon when iconSvg is a non-empty string
PluginIcon falls back to letter avatar when iconSvg is empty string
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/NpmPluginCard.tsx (pass icon props)

Context:
PluginIcon already accepts optional iconSvg, iconInactiveSvg, iconDarkSvg, iconDarkInactiveSvg props (see PluginIconProps lines 56-67 in PluginIcon.tsx). When iconSvg is provided and non-empty, it renders the sanitized SVG. When absent or empty, it falls back to the letter avatar. No changes needed to PluginIcon itself.

For search results, we only have light and dark active icons (no inactive variants) since search results don't have active/inactive states.

Changes:
Update line 22 in NpmPluginCard.tsx from:
```tsx
<PluginIcon pluginName={plugin.name} displayName={displayName} tabState="closed" size={28} />
```
to:
```tsx
<PluginIcon
  pluginName={plugin.name}
  displayName={displayName}
  tabState="closed"
  size={28}
  iconSvg={plugin.iconSvg || undefined}
  iconDarkSvg={plugin.iconDarkSvg || undefined}
/>
```

Note: Pass undefined (not empty string) when there's no icon, since PluginIcon checks `!!iconSvg` (truthy) to decide whether to render SVG or avatar. Empty string is falsy so it would work, but `|| undefined` is explicit.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change PluginIcon.tsx — it already handles all the rendering logic.
- Do NOT pass iconInactiveSvg or iconDarkInactiveSvg — search results don't need inactive states.
- The tabState remains 'closed' — search results are not installed plugins.

userStories.title: Add E2E test verifying marketplace search results display plugin icons
userStories.description: Add E2E tests that verify search results render actual SVG icons from the npm registry instead of letter-avatar fallbacks. Test both the presence of icons and graceful fallback when icons are missing.
acceptanceCriteria: New E2E test verifies that marketplace search result cards display SVG icons (not just letter avatars) for plugins that have icons published to npm
Test checks that the PluginIcon container within NpmPluginCard contains an SVG element when the plugin has icons on npm
Test verifies the icon changes between light and dark mode (different SVG content rendered)
Test verifies that plugins without icons still render the letter-avatar fallback gracefully
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts (add new test cases)

Context:
Existing tests in side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts already test the search → install → uninstall flow. The test setup pattern is consistent: create isolated configDir, start MCP server, launch extension context, open side panel, interact.

The tests depend on npm search returning results (checked by npmSearchFindsPlugins helper). Tests that depend on npm search are wrapped in `test.skip(!searchAvailable, ...)`. Follow this same pattern.

All @opentabs-dev plugins now have iconSvg and iconDarkSvg in their package.json opentabs field (published to npm). The e2e-test plugin is a local plugin and its icons are in tools.json (rendered differently by the installed plugin card), but npm search results come from the registry.

Key selectors and patterns from existing tests:
- `sidePanelPage.getByPlaceholder('Search plugins and tools...')` — search input
- `sidePanelPage.getByText('Available')` — npm results section header
- `sidePanelPage.getByRole('button', { name: 'Install' }).first()` — install button on first result

For icon verification, the NpmPluginCard renders PluginIcon which either:
1. Has SVG icon: renders `<div ... dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: svgToRender }} />` — the inner div will contain an `<svg>` element
2. No SVG icon: renders a `<span>` with the letter avatar and a colored background

So to verify an icon is rendered as SVG, check for an `svg` element inside the icon container. The first NpmPluginCard in search results for common queries like 'slack' or 'discord' should have SVG icons since all published plugins now embed them.

Changes:

1. Add test 'search results display SVG icons from npm registry' inside the existing 'search-dependent tests' describe block:
   - Fill search with 'slack' (a plugin known to have icons)
   - Wait for Available section to appear
   - Find the first NpmPluginCard in the Available section
   - Assert that the icon container (the first child div of the card) contains an `<svg>` element
   - Use a locator like: `sidePanelPage.locator('.space-y-2.rounded.border-2').first().locator('svg').first()` or scope within the Available section

2. Add test 'search result icons change between light and dark mode':
   - Fill search with 'slack'
   - Wait for Available section
   - Capture the innerHTML of the icon SVG in light mode
   - Toggle dark mode (click the dark mode toggle — `sidePanelPage.getByLabel('Switch to dark mode')` or check if there's a specific toggle)
   - Capture the innerHTML of the icon SVG in dark mode
   - Assert the two are different (the dark variant has adjusted colors, e.g., #4F46E5 → #5b52e7 for e2e-test)
   - NOTE: The dark mode toggle is in the side panel footer. Check the existing dark mode E2E test in side-panel-theme.e2e.ts for the exact selector pattern.

3. For fallback testing, this is harder to test against live npm since all plugins have icons. One approach: assert that if no `svg` is found in a card, a `span` with a single letter exists (the avatar fallback). But since all plugins have icons now, this might not be testable against live npm. Consider just testing the positive case (icons render) and skip the fallback test — the unit tests for PluginIcon already cover the fallback path.

Constraints:
- Follow the exact same test setup/teardown pattern as existing tests in this file.
- Use `test.slow()` for tests that hit npm.
- All tests must be inside the 'search-dependent tests' describe block with the `test.skip(!searchAvailable, ...)` guard.
- Use `test.skip(!searchAvailable, ...)` at the describe level (already done for the existing block).
- Do NOT mock npm — these are E2E tests that hit the real npm registry.
- Use robust selectors — don't rely on exact CSS class names that might change. Prefer aria roles and semantic selectors.
- The dark mode toggle: read side-panel-theme.e2e.ts first to find the exact selector for toggling dark mode. The toggle might be `getByLabel('Switch to dark mode')` or similar.
